Veqtor v0.1.1 is an Alpha reliability update for local DOCX negotiation
workflows.

This release makes decision-record export semantics self-describing: the live
response now distinguishes substantive compact records, locally written access
events and the snapshot used by each count. The installed-wheel and manual
Claude Desktop gates both exercise two consecutive exports. A closed,
path-free acceptance packet binds both live gates and their runtime identities
to the exact promoted source tree and seals its canonical bytes with an
explicitly approved SHA-256.

Release promotion now recovers interrupted drafts through authenticated release
listing and release-id operations, while failing closed on an ambiguous tag or
unexpected asset state. Documentation also clarifies reinstate behavior,
compact clause-label digests and provenance side effects of read-only calls.

This remains a local technical Alpha, not a hosted service, legal-advice system,
general-purpose Word editor or tamper-evident audit log. Review the
[known limitations](https://github.com/JohnDeer-ai/veqtor-mcp/blob/v0.1.1/KNOWN_LIMITATIONS.md)
before using it on a matter. Keep source documents and raw `.veqtor` journals
private.
